note_en:
This story is available exclusively to Insider subscribers.  Become an Insider and start reading now.
  Google's healthcare group is moving more than 100 employees into other areas of the company, according to three current and former Alphabet employees, and an internal memo seen by Insider. The employees were not authorized to speak to the press.
 More than 130 employees inside Google Health have been moved under Search and the newly-acquired Fitbit group. Those remaining at Google Health have been consolidated into three teams, none of which will focus on consumer-facing products.
 In March, Google Health had around 700 people working on research, imaging, clinical tools, health sensors, and more. That number has dropped to almost 570 as of this week – a near-20% cut to its headcount – according to internal data obtained by Insider.
 The changes should improve the group's impact and execution speed, Dr. David Feinberg, the head of Google Health, said in an email on May 5 seen by Insider. They should also support Google's broader work at a time when the company is expanding its focus on health and wellness, he said.
 "We brought together some teams to combine expertise, and focus our efforts on health and wellness," a spokesperson for Google Health said in a statement to Insider. "Google Health will continue to build products for clinicians, conduct research to improve care and make people healthier, and to help ensure all health-related projects at Google meet the highest standards."
 The division was created by a company-wide effort to combine many of Alphabet's disparate health projects under Feinberg  in 2018. Its mission has been to make people's health information broadly accessible and useful in a world where finding a doctor, analyzing clinical data, and diagnosing conditions can be made easier by technology.
 But Google has run into roadblocks,  many of them internal, in the years since. A deal between the tech giant and the health system Ascension, inked in 2018, stirred a  public uproar and a federal investigation over the amount of patient medical records it gave Google access to. For the majority of time since, addressing the coronavirus pandemic has been a  big focus of Google Health's work.
 Reorganizations are common at Google, but in recent years the company  has made a more concerted effort to consolidate parts of the business and increase collaboration between teams. The call to restructure Google Health did not originate with Feinberg, a former employee said.
     The spokesperson for Google Health said that organizational decisions of Google Health were made by Feinberg and his leadership team.
    With more than  29 million users, Fitbit is known for its fitness trackers. It also has coaching services and works with  health plans, health systems, and other  industry groups.
 Google's Fitbit acquisition gives the tech giant more muscle to take on Apple and other rivals in consumer health hardware. The Fitbit team is now a part of Google's "devices and services" division, led by Rick Osterloh, a senior vice president.
 As Google Health increasingly took on mobile tools such as sensors that identify skin conditions and  measure heart rates, understanding how the Fitbit acquisition would fit in has been a big question.
  Fitbit is absorbing the teams working on health sensors and  personal health records as well as some of its leaders in business development, per Feinberg's note and changes to several employees' LinkedIn profiles.
     Shwetak Patel, the former director of health technologies at Google Health, is joining Fitbit's research team, for example, and will continue his work in health sensing in partnership with the newly instituted AI team at Google Health, Feinberg said in the email.
 Another group is moving to Google Search to create a new "health journeys" vertical, Feinberg said.
 The group will continue many projects that sprung out of the coronavirus pandemic, such as creating a way to help people search for  doctors that provide virtual care and get screened for mental health conditions.
  Google Health has also consolidated its remaining employees into three teams, Feinberg announced in the memo.
 One is focused on work with clinicians, led by Paul Muret, a vice president, the email said. The team will focus on building Google Health's flagship product, Care Studio, a Google-like search capability for electronic health records. The program is still being piloted with the Missouri-based Ascension and,  as of this Spring, Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center.
 The second team, called "Health AI," will spearhead innovation in health, including in medical imaging, the email said. It'll be the new home for Google's genomics work and tool for screening diabetic retinopathy with an algorithm, one of Google Health's most advanced ideas, among others.
 Google Health's third and final group, joined by clinicians from Fitbit and Google Fit, will deal with regulatory, clinical, and health equity matters at Google Health and across the broader company. It'll provide "embedded support" to teams like Fitbit and others, Feinberg said.
 In recent years, many big tech companies have created centralized "industry" teams for healthcare, but now there seems to be a trend in the other direction towards selling consumer-grade apps, devices, and similarly "light" efforts, Dr. Jonathan Slotkin, chief medical officer at Contigo, a healthcare startup at Premier, told Insider.
 "It makes me think that in the balance between focused health industry expertise and core company businesses, the core company businesses are winning," he said.

note:
Nylas，API（应用程序编程接口）平台，可帮助业务和开发人员将电子邮件和日历功能等关键生产率功能集成到其应用中，由Tiger Global Management领导的C系列资金筹集了1.2亿美元。
 在蓬勃发展的API经济中，加剧是通过如何通过点击更广泛的数字生态系统来提高其效率的API经济来实现的。企业可以使用由第三方创建的目的内部API来消费自己的基础架构，而不是消费内部资源，而是通过第三方创建的目的地API，以将登录身份验证给他们的财务应用程序，隐私到他们的数据饥饿的医疗保健应用程序，或者客户服务支持他们的电子商务应用程序。
   Nylas提供了API跨越电子邮件，日历，联系人和调度，并包括与Microsoft，Google，AOL，雅虎和IMAP相似的所有主要服务的集成。因此，而不是必须为所有这些不同的服务开发多个集成，而是需要持续维护和管理的资源密集型努力，一个CRM软件制造商（例如）可以使用Nylas的电子邮件API来绕过所有这些障碍让销售团队发送和销售团队发送直接从CRM接收来自任何提供商的电子邮件。
 在其客户端基础中是云通信Juggernaut Dialpad，它使用Nylas来提供其最终用户的完整创建，读取，更新和删除（CRUD）功能跨越电子邮件，日历和联系人。这确保了所有数据在拨号板和服务提供商帐户之间的两个方向上不断同步（例如Google或Microsoft）。
   在过去的一年里，尼腹已经推出了一个新的神经API，它在去年3月份的第一次收购中源于june.ai，它是一个可自动组织用户的收件箱的AI启用的电子邮件客户端。作为尼布斯的一部分，该技术有助于聚合，分析和处理从电子邮件，日历和联系人收集的巨大数据，采取非结构化数据并将其转换为更具可用的东西。
 “Nylas神经API采用非结构化数据，并使用智能和机器学习算法来触发每周一个全天的普通人的自动化，”尼布斯Cofounder和Ceo Gleb Polyakov告诉Venturebeat。
 例如，“分类程序”将机器到人类与人类消息区分开来，同时还检测到反弹电子邮件和自动外出响应。 
“客户使用此功能基于检测Out Off Out Out Out Emails自动暂停销售节奏，并根据电子邮件中的日期重新启动它们，”Polyakov解释说。 “客户还使用分类程序API来触发企业特定的自动化 - 例如，电子商务客户使用分类程序检测运输确认相关电子邮件，并向客户发送自定义通知。”
 在其他地方，神经API可以推断电子邮件签名中找到的关键数据，以自动更新他们的地址簿，甚至根据职位变更触发工作流程，而情感分析可帮助用户弄清电子邮件是积极的，负面或中性的。和“清洁对话”有助于从消息中过滤掉“非必要”内容，以便到谈话的螺母和螺栓。
 “电子邮件通常污染且难以阅读，因为它们包含过去的响应，签名，法律语言等，”Polyakov表示“清洁对话API使开发人员可以轻松访问任何电子邮件的最重要部分 - 身体 - 通过单个API呼叫。“
   在现在之前，Nylas已经筹集了5500万美元，其中几乎一半通过其第6月通过其系列。其最新投资包括一系列名人的旧和新投资者，包括松弛（通过Slack Fund）;条纹Cofounders Patrick和John Collicon;巢创始人Tony Fadell; Klarna首席执行官Sebastian Siemiatkowski;花旗资企业; 8Vc;第13轮资本;和猫头鹰岩石资本。
 尼斯表示，在银行额外12 000万美元表示，它计划继续投资其产品，包括安全，组件和自动化，如AI和ML，情感分析，自然语言处理（NLP）等。
 Polyakov表示，“已经开始建立现代软件，以建立现代软件，为建立现代软件进行建立现代软件，挖掘工作流程，协调工作流程和建立丰富的前端用户体验，”Polyakov表示。 “我们正在开发所有这些领域的产品和服务，以帮助开发人员销售价值更快到最终客户。”

Nylas, an API (application programming interface) platform that helps businesses and developers integrate key productivity features such as email and calendar functionality into their apps, has raised $120 million in a series C round of funding led by Tiger Global Management.
 The raise comes amid a  booming API economy, defined by how organizations are using APIs to improve their efficiency by tapping the wider digital ecosystem. Rather than consuming internal resources developing their own infrastructure from scratch, businesses can use purpose-built APIs created by third-parties to bring  login authentication to their finance app,  privacy to their data-hungry health care app, or  customer service support to their ecommerce app.
   Nylas offers APIs spanning email, calendar, contacts, and scheduling, and includes integrations with all the major services from the likes of Microsoft, Google, AOL, Yahoo, and IMAP. So rather than having to develop multiple integrations for all these different services, a resource-intensive endeavor that requires ongoing maintenance and management, a CRM software maker (for example) can use Nylas’s email API to bypass all these hurdles to let sales teams send and receive emails from any provider directly from the CRM.
 Among its client base is  cloud communications juggernaut Dialpad, which uses Nylas to offer its end-users full create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) functionality spanning email, calendar, and contacts. This ensures all data is constantly synchronized in both directions between Dialpad and the service provider accounts (e.g. Google or Microsoft).
   In the past year, Nylas has launched a new  neural API, which stems from its first  acquisition last March when it snapped up  June.ai, an  AI-enabled email client that automatically organizes a user’s inbox. As part of Nylas, the technology helps to aggregate, analyze, and process vast swathes of data gleaned from emails, calendars, and contacts, taking unstructured data and transforming it into something more usable.
 “The Nylas neural API takes unstructured data, and uses intelligence and machine-learning algorithms to trigger automations that can save the average person one full day’s worth of work each week,” Nylas cofounder and CEO Gleb Polyakov told VentureBeat.
 For example, “categorizer” distinguishes machine-to-human from human-to-human messages, while also detecting bounced emails and automated out-of-office responses.
 “Customers use this feature to automatically pause sales cadences based on detecting out-of-office emails and restart them based on the dates found in the email,” Polyakov explained. “Customers also use the categorizer API to trigger business specific automations — for instance, ecommerce customers use a categorizer to detect a shipping confirmation related email, and send custom notifications to their customers.”
 Elsewhere, the neural API can extrapolate key data found in email signatures to automatically update their address books, or even trigger workflows based on a job title change, while sentiment analytics helps users figure out whether an email was positive, negative, or neutral. And ‘clean conversations’ helps to filter out “non-essential” content from messages to get to the nuts and bolts of a conversation.
 “Emails are often polluted and hard to read through since they contain past responses, signatures, legal language, and so on,” Polyakov said “The clean conversations API makes it easy for developers to access the most important part of any email — the body — via a single API call.”
   Prior to now, Nylas had raised around $55 million, almost half of which came via its  series B round last June. Its latest investment included a host of notable old and new investors, including Slack (via Slack Fund); Stripe cofounders Patrick and John Collison; Nest founder Tony Fadell; Klarna CEO Sebastian Siemiatkowski; Citi Ventures; 8VC; Round13 Capital; and Owl Rock Capital.
 With another $120 million in the bank, Nylas said that it plans to continue investing in its product including security, components, and automation, such as AI and ML, sentiment analysis, natural language processing (NLP), and more.
 “There’s a lot of work a developer has to do to build modern software –everything from integrating backend APIs, mining and filtering data, orchestrating workflows, and building rich front-end user experiences,” Polyakov said. “We’re developing products and services across all of these areas to help developers ship value faster to their end customers.”

Claroty是一款网络安全技术的开发商，专注于业务的工业方面，周四公布了一轮新一轮的资金，这可能是在看待IPO之前的最后一个。
 随着新系列的D型资金，纽约公司现在已经积累了2.35亿美元。
 领导这一轮是两家新投资者，白宫企业合作伙伴世纪的II基金和40北，纽约全球工业公司标准行业的投资臂。另一个战略投资者，基于韩国的LG，也加入了这一轮。他们加入在Claroty的其他战略投资者，包括罗克韦尔自动化，西门子和施耐德电气。
  Coloty不像大多数其他网络安全公司，因为它侧重于业务的工业方面，首席执行官Yaniv Vardi表示。 Vardi告诉CRN，它在包括汽车，生命科学，食品和饮料，以及保护物联网和工业物联网的关键基础设施以及安全远程访问，以及安全远程访问。
 “工业经济正在经历数字转型，变得更加富有成效，”他说。 “他们正在服用遗留系统，这可能是空闲的，并连接它们。”
 Vardi表示，Claroty的主要差异是其研究团队，大多数人来自以色列国防部队的精英单位，领先的运营技术，或卖家，包括一些公司的投资者，包括一些公司的投资者。
 “我们可以通过施耐德电气，罗克韦尔自动化和西门子的帮助来反转工程师遥替，并使用它们，”他说。 “这三者也使用和销售我们的产品。它不仅仅是ot，也是IoT和IIT。我们在工业方面的四墙上保护一切。“ 
Vardi引用作为客户端辉瑞的示例，顶级Covid-19疫苗开发人员之一。 “辉瑞说，它保护世界，”他说。 “我们保护辉瑞。”
 Vardi说，Claroty还有一个大型合作伙伴生态系统，除了也销售其技术的战略投资者外，还有一位销售其技术。
 它包括服务提供商，如Deloitte，NTT数据，埃森哲和Kudelski集团，多个自动化技术开发商和全球较小的渠道合作伙伴。而且，对于寻找IT网络安全的客户来说，Claroty还与以色列的检查点软件技术和加利福尼亚州的以色列的检查点软件技术和Sunnyvale等提供商合作。
 Vardi说，新一轮资金将用于帮助Claroty扩大其进入更广泛的工业网络安全领域，并加深其域名经验。
 “例如，识别像智能摄像机这样的物联网设备很容易，”他说。 “很多公司都这样做。但是，我们允许客户端单击并获得网络上所有设备的完全可见性，并在云中映射风险。“
  “但我们正在寻求成长并扩大，”他说。 “我们有一个新的垂直和专业知识的汇总策略。例如，我们希望在不同的垂直方面获得更多的IOT功能。“
 Vardi说，Claroty没有积极地寻求获得IT网络安全的专业知识，但正在考虑选择。 
“我们认为这是一个预见的圆形，”他说。 “我们不指望另一轮。 我们尚未有利可图。 相反，我们正在寻求使用收益不盈利，而是为了扩展到额外的垂直和地理位置。“
note_en:
Claroty, a developer of cybersecurity technology focused on the industrial side of the business, on Thursday unveiled the closing of a new round of funding that will likely be the last one before looking at an IPO.
 With the new Series D round of funding, the New York-based company now has amassed a total of $235 million.
 Leading this round are two new investors, Bessemer Venture Partners’ Century II fund and 40 North, the investment arm of New York-based global industrial company Standard Industries. Another strategic investor, Korea-based LG, also joined this round. They join other strategic investors in  Claroty, including Rockwell Automation, Siemens, and Schneider Electric.
  Claroty is not like most other cybersecurity companies in that it has a focus on the industrial side of the business, said  CEO Yaniv Vardi. It works across industrial verticals including automotive, life sciences, food and beverage, and critical infrastructure to protect IoT and industrial IoT, or IIoT, as well as secure remote access, Vardi told CRN.
 “The industrial economy is going through digital transformation to become more productive,” he said. “They are taking legacy systems, which were likely air-gapped, and connecting them.”
 Claroty’s primary difference is how its research team, most of whom are from elite units of the Israel Defense Forces, partners with leading operations technology, or OT, vendors including some of the company’s investors, Vardi said.
 “We are able to reverse-engineer OT protocols and use them thanks to help from Schneider Electric, Rockwell Automation, and Siemens,” he said. “These three also use and sell our products. And it’s not just OT, but also IoT and IIoT. We protect everything in the four walls on the industrial side.”
 Vardi cited as an example of a client Pfizer, one of the top  COVID-19 vaccine developers. “Pfizer says it protects the world,” he said. “We protect Pfizer.”
 Claroty has a large partner ecosystem in addition to the strategic investors who also sell its technology, Vardi said.
 It includes services providers such as Deloitte, NTT Data, Accenture, and Kudelski Group, several automation technology developers, and smaller channel partners worldwide. And, for clients looking for IT cybersecurity, Claroty also partners with providers such as Israel-based Check Point Software Technologies and Sunnyvale, Calif.-based CrowdStrike, he said.
 The new round of funding will be used to help Claroty both broaden its reach into a wider range of industrial cybersecurity areas such as IoT, and deepen its domain experience, Vardi said.
 “For example, identifying IoT devices like smart cameras is easy,” he said. “A lot of companies do that. But we allow the client to double-click and get full visibility of all devices on the network and map the risks, all over the cloud.”
  “But we are looking to grow and expand,” he said. “We have a roll-up strategy for both new verticals and expertise. For example, we would like to get more IoT capabilities in different verticals.”
 Claroty is not actively looking to acquire expertise in IT cybersecurity, but is considering the options, Vardi said.
  “We consider it as a pre-IPO round,” he said. “We don’t expect another round. We are not profitable yet. Instead, we are looking to use proceeds not to grow profitable but to expand significantly into additional verticals and geographies.”
